questions.question: Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Reliable
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Admiration
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Rukhsana was left alone in the house again, but fear was now her <u>constant</u> companion.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Concur’ from the given sentence. Every time they met, he found it difficult to resist her odd magic.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Admonish

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in the bracket to fill in the blank. The development of the region also depended on the ________ (rapaciousness) of the corporate contribution via a social responsibility scheme.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Amplify
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The <u>amateur</u> proved them all wrong.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. This <u>narrow</u> lane will take you to your destination.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Exaggerate
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Fathomable
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Inclusion

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the bracketed word in the following sentence to fill in the blank. The patient consumed mushrooms for hallucinatory experiences, but the doctor advised to consume only ________(hallucinogenic) drugs.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. His was a <u>stupendous</u> performance in the competition.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. The team’s performance was at its <u>zenith</u> in 2017.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. She made a <u>courageous</u> decision to quit her job.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the bracketed word in the following sentence to fill in the blank. My friend is not stubborn with regard to opinions; in fact, she is ________(intransigent).
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Latent
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word to fill in the blank. A true friend always _______, but a selfish friend tries to <u>mislead</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word to fill in the blank. Only Mr. Sen’s emotions were <u>genuine</u>, while others showed ___________ sadness at grandmother’s death.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence given below. Do not <u>linger</u> in the corridor.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Languishing
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Endless
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Deplore
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence. Dipesh loves to answer his boss’s questions in <u>tactful</u> ways.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Prefer
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. His <u>gloomy</u> demeanour is often criticised.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. His request was <u>overlooked</u> by the management.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Inquisitive
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Mutely
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence. She looked tired and <u>sallow</u> after the long day she’d had.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. Amrit felt a chill run down his spine as Mrityu <u>gazed</u> at him mid-conversation.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The enemy, even if it is a child, is a <u>challenge</u> and needs to be removed from his path.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM to replace the italicised word in the given sentence. I am astonished to see her mercurial demeanour in making any task a success.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Intent
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. This book is written by a <u>famous</u> author named G.H. Sullivan.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word to fill in the blank. We are blessed with a <u>prudent</u> house keeper, but cursed with an ________ house master.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Virtue’ from the given sentence. Every person's actions have an innate power that determines the appropriate reward or punishment in addition to a moral value that produces merit or demerit.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. The detective's <u>astute</u> observations led to the discovery of crucial evidence, unlike his oblivious partner.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Stretch
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Accelerate
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Curiosity
